content.text:
---
name: browse
description: |
  Browse and interact with web pages using Chrome DevTools MCP. Use this skill when:
  (1) Navigating to URLs to inspect web pages
  (2) Taking screenshots of web pages
  (3) Clicking elements, filling forms, or interacting with UI
  (4) Inspecting DOM, console logs, or network requests
  (5) Debugging frontend issues or testing web applications
  (6) Verifying UI rendering or behavior
  Requires Chrome running with --remote-debugging-port=9222
---

# Browse

Control Chrome browser via DevTools Protocol for web inspection, testing, and debugging.

## Prerequisites

Chrome must be running with remote debugging enabled:

```bash
# Linux
google-chrome --remote-debugging-port=9222

# macOS
/Applications/Google\ Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google\ Chrome --remote-debugging-port=9222
```

## Available Tools

The chrome-devtools MCP provides:

### Navigation
- `devtools_list_tabs` - List open browser tabs
- `devtools_new_tab` - Open new tab with URL
- `devtools_navigate` - Navigate current tab to URL
- `devtools_close_tab` - Close a tab

### Content Capture
- `devtools_snapshot` - Get accessibility tree snapshot (structured DOM)
- `devtools_screenshot` - Capture screenshot as PNG
- `devtools_evaluate` - Execute JavaScript and get result

### Interaction
- `devtools_click` - Click element by selector or coordinates
- `devtools_type` - Type text into focused element
- `devtools_fill` - Fill form field by selector

### Debugging
- `devtools_console_messages` - Get console log messages
- `devtools_network_requests` - Monitor network activity

## Workflow

1. Verify Chrome is running: `devtools_list_tabs`
2. Navigate: `devtools_navigate` or `devtools_new_tab`
3. Inspect: `devtools_snapshot`
4. Interact: `devtools_click`, `devtools_type`, `devtools_fill`
5. Capture: `devtools_screenshot`

## Tips

- Use `devtools_snapshot` before clicking to understand page structure
- Element selectors use CSS syntax (`#id`, `.class`, `button[type="submit"]`)
- Console messages help debug JavaScript errors
- Network requests help debug API issues

---
name: browse
description: |
  Browse and interact with web pages using Chrome DevTools MCP. Use this skill when:
  (1) Navigating to URLs to inspect web pages
  (2) Taking screenshots of web pages
  (3) Clicking elements, filling forms, or interacting with UI
  (4) Inspecting DOM, console logs, or network requests
  (5) Debugging frontend issues or testing web applications
  (6) Verifying UI rendering or behavior
  Requires Chrome running with --remote-debugging-port=9222
---

# Browse

Control Chrome browser via DevTools Protocol for web inspection, testing, and debugging.

## Prerequisites

Chrome must be running with remote debugging enabled:

```bash
# Linux
google-chrome --remote-debugging-port=9222

# macOS
/Applications/Google\ Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google\ Chrome --remote-debugging-port=9222
```

## Available Tools

The chrome-devtools MCP provides:

### Navigation
- `devtools_list_tabs` - List open browser tabs
- `devtools_new_tab` - Open new tab with URL
- `devtools_navigate` - Navigate current tab to URL
- `devtools_close_tab` - Close a tab

### Content Capture
- `devtools_snapshot` - Get accessibility tree snapshot (structured DOM)
- `devtools_screenshot` - Capture screenshot as PNG
- `devtools_evaluate` - Execute JavaScript and get result

### Interaction
- `devtools_click` - Click element by selector or coordinates
- `devtools_type` - Type text into focused element
- `devtools_fill` - Fill form field by selector

### Debugging
- `devtools_console_messages` - Get console log messages
- `devtools_network_requests` - Monitor network activity

## Workflow

1. Verify Chrome is running: `devtools_list_tabs`
2. Navigate: `devtools_navigate` or `devtools_new_tab`
3. Inspect: `devtools_snapshot`
4. Interact: `devtools_click`, `devtools_type`, `devtools_fill`
5. Capture: `devtools_screenshot`

## Tips

- Use `devtools_snapshot` before clicking to understand page structure
- Element selectors use CSS syntax (`#id`, `.class`, `button[type="submit"]`)
- Console messages help debug JavaScript errors
- Network requests help debug API issues
